name: Berry Syrup
recipeIngredient: Fresh or frozen raspberries (black or red)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and prepare the fruit, removing stems and caps.
2. Crush the fruit in a saucepan until smooth.
3. Heat to boiling and simmer for 5 to 10 minutes until soft.
4. Strain hot juice through a colander into a clean container.
5. Allow to cool slightly.
6. Strain collected juice again using double layers of cheesecloth or jelly bag.
7. Discard the pulp.
8. The pressed juice should yield about 4-1/2 to 5 cups.
9. In a large saucepan, combine with 6-3/4 cups sugar and bring to boil.
10. Simmer for 1 minute.
11. To make whole fruit syrup, save 1 or 2 cups of crushed fruit and add to the sugar mixture.
12. Remove from heat, skim foam, and fill clean half-pint or pint jars with juice.
13. Leave 1/2-inch headspace and seal.
14. Process according to Table 1 recommendations.
Table 1. Recommended process time for Berry Syrup in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Hot. Jar Size: Half-pints or Pints.
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 min.
1,001 - 6,000 ft: 15 min.
Above 6,000 ft: 20 min.
